matches.evidence: Classic read-then-write race. reminder.retryCount was fetched at handler start. If the cron overlaps or fires twice, two executions read retryCount: 0, both write retryCount: 1, and you lose an increment. Use Prisma's atomic increment: { increment: 1 }
matches.reasoning: The review identifies the exact same defect — stale read of retryCount leading to lost increments under concurrency — and prescribes the same fix (Prisma atomic increment), noting it applies to both update calls.

matches.evidence: The second OR branch has no method filter. It will delete any WorkflowReminder with retryCount > 1 — SMS, email, whatever else gets added in the future.
matches.reasoning: The review explicitly identifies that the retryCount > 1 deletion condition lacks a method: WorkflowMethods.SMS filter, causing it to delete non-SMS reminders, which is the exact defect described in the golden bug.
